title: pelargonium × clintoniae
intro: Pelargonium × clintoniae, also known as the Cape Barren, is a hybrid species of flowering plant that is part of the Geraniaceae family. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pelargonium × clintoniae include relieving symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as providing relief from fever and sore throats. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in folk medicine to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
